presentation.brandSurface: canonical-rich-deck-gradient on the right panel; chart lives in a translucent glass card
presentation.chartGeometry: THE BARS ARE CSS WIDTHS, NOT DATA. The chart cannot be filled with text alone — see resolvers.chart-bar-geometry. If the resolver is not applied, the composer MUST abort the slide: writing the labels without recomputing the widths produces a chart that lies.

contract: scale = 88 / max(valuePct across items); each item's bar gets style.width = round(valuePct * scale) + '%' so the largest series lands exactly on the 88% reference line
.refline stays at left:88% (it marks the leading series); if the resolver changes the scale, it must move the refline to the same percentage as the largest bar
item.highlight = 'sky' adds class 'sky' to the .row (teal label + glowing teal fill) and 'sky' to .fill; 'muted' keeps class 'muted' on .fill — no other tones exist

the highlighted row is the only one that carries .gap: style.left = highlighted bar width %, style.width = (largest bar width % − highlighted bar width %); the resolver removes .gap from every non-highlighted row and also when highlighted is the leader (gap = 0)
.glabel text (gapCallout) must be derived from the same numbers (e.g. '+32 pts a cerrar' = leader.valuePct − highlighted.valuePct); it must never be authored free-hand out of sync with valuePct
item.value (the printed label, e.g. '50%') is text and stays under data-slot-field='value', but the resolver must assert it is consistent with valuePct before rendering
